author: @TheHelloki

text:
Hold on a sec.  Everything under the umbrella of "YouTube Poop" is technically "stolen pictures", and you're concerned about a few watermarked stock photos?  Granted, you're not *wrong*, and I should consider licensing content for the future.  But I do think of it as Fair Use since 1) I'm leaving the watermark on, and 2) each image is used for a very short amount of time.  I firmly believe in freedom to use and remix any and all content made available over the internet, and this will never change.  You have to be selective for the things you really want to pay for.

FYI, I don't make enough from Patreon to justify paying for stock photos that I'll only probably be using once.  $12 for ONE photo??  That's highway robbery.  Sure, a corporation can afford that easily 'cause they've already got big bucks.  But if I paid for every photo here, that would come to well over $100.  I already pay $60/mo to Adobe, then $100+ more to other creators, plus Patreon taxes/fees.  If you think I'm just being greedy here, you are greatly mistaken.  I can barely afford to live.  Maybe in the future this will change, and I can appropriately pay for every little thing I use in YTPs.  But I can't give money that I don't have.  Just because I have a decent-size audience doesn't mean anything, and I think that's a grave misconception.  You can be widely known and appreciated but still die penniless.

Merry Christmas to you as well, and thank you for being a fan!
author: @cs188

text:
@cs188  Yes and I agree with that, and I’m also not saying the copyright system is fair, I’m just saying that as someone that knows how hard it is to become a full-time photographer, I just like fairness. I’d also love if more royalty-free content was available for use, but that’s the world we live in. 

See you!
